item.title: Junior Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at any time.

The WikiWalk Team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ show block
– hide block

This one is self-contained, but it does mention a GoI and the hub page for that GoI can be linked.

Editing this:

Addendum: 47 instances of SCP-2829 were recovered from the basement of an abandoned home in ███████, TX, along with the corpse of the house's owner, Dr. Javier Pineda, a former Prometheus Labs employee.

To this:

Addendum: 47 instances of SCP-2829 were recovered from the basement of an abandoned home in ███████, TX, along with the corpse of the house's owner, Dr. Javier Pineda, a former Prometheus Labs employee.

If anyone has any other ideas, feel free to suggest them below.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just noticed that this page has been translated for the Chinese branch by the wonderful LeoOverheat! It can be viewed here. Thanks!

item.title: Backstory! Woo!
item.content:encoded:
Funny thing, when I first started working on the original concept for this (three or four overhauls ago), the similarity to the Adipose was the first thing people brought up. I hadn't heard of them before that day, and I've tried my darndest to differentiate from that concept, but when the goal is 'happy fat', there's only so much you can do.

The original seed of this idea came from my little brother, who hasn't seen any Dr. Who either, for what it's worth, but the end result is a lot different than what he came up with.

Glad you liked it! And I might write more about these things when I have both motivation, and an idea at the same time.

item.title: Happy Fat
item.content:encoded: I like this. I +1-ed it. But, while reading this, I kept thinking of the Adipose blobs from Dr Who. Not exactly the same, but still in the same happy fat family of cute little creatures. We need more of the happy fat.

item.title: better late than never
item.content:encoded:
Please accept this gift from a fellow procrastinator:

see this is why I stick to writing instead of art
WHAT DOES THE SLUG SAY

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I procrastinated actually commenting on this article, but I do really like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I generally don't like convenient journals of exposition, but yours had enough personality for me to overlook it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Done.

Also,

weight loss clinic filled with these things

really wish I thought of that one :P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'd say keep them the same and just remove the footnotes on organelles and endoplastic reticulum.

@DrLevy

I imagine the people who work at the Foundation are pretty smart, and know what they are.

Keep in mind that SCP documentation is intended as a standard briefing file: New researchers assigned to an object, the MTF that's about to go try and clear out a weight loss clinic filled with these things, the budget committee that needs to know why site 74 listed "3 L containers of lard (14x)" on last month's emergency expenses report… all sorts of people in-universe could end up needing to read a file.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hmm, maybe you're right about the footnotes. Maybe I could just say "fat cells" instead of "adipocytes", and then remove footnotes 2 and 3?

Made it 'smooth ER' (haha), thanks. Glad you liked it :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this, but are the footnotes describing what adipocytes, organelles, and endoplasmic reticulum are really necessary? I understand that not everyone who reads them understand what they mean, but it ruins the immersion a little bit, because I imagine the people who work at the Foundation are pretty smart, and know what they are.

Also, just another little biology term, you said that endoplasmic reticulums synthesise lipids; you're partially right, if you want to be more technical, say that it's the smooth endoplasmic reticulum, which is the one that synthesises lipids. The other one, the rough endoplasmic reticulum, is responsible for the synthesis of proteins (via attached ribosomes), and packaging of said proteins.

Other than that, very good first SCP, take my upvote :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is pretty cool. I especially like the end, suggesting there are more out there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked it when I read it as a draft, I still like it now. It's cute but sinister, and it just fits. +1

item.title: Finally
item.content:encoded:
Well. My first SCP is up. Took me long enough :)

Huge thanks to Zyn and SpectalDragon on the forums, as well as GibberingEloquence, Sliptide213, DrBleep, AbsentmindedNihilist, and everyone else on chat that helped out and looked over it (if I forgot your name I'm sorry, let me know).

https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Two_Banana_Slugs.jpg public domain
